questions.question: Wat betekent Kanban letterlijk?
questions.explanation: Kanban (看板) is Japanese for "visual signal" or "card".
questions.explanationNL: Kanban (看板) is Japans voor "visueel signaal" of "kaart".

questions.question: Wat is Little's Law?
questions.explanation: Little's Law states that Lead Time = WIP / Throughput. Reducing Work In Progress (WIP) directly reduces lead time when throughput is held constant.
questions.explanationNL: Little's Law stelt dat Lead Time = WIP / Throughput.

questions.question: Waarom zijn WIP limieten pijnlijk?
options: Because they are set too high
Because they expose problems that need to be solved
optionsEN: Because they are set too high
Because they expose problems that need to be solved
optionsNL: Omdat ze problemen onthullen die je moet oplossen
questions.explanation: Work In Progress (WIP) limits are intentionally painful — the discomfort reveals bottlenecks, dependencies, and imbalances in capacity that must be addressed to improve flow.
questions.explanationNL: WIP limieten zijn bedoeld om pijnlijk te zijn - ze onthullen bottlenecks en problemen.

questions.text: What does a Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD) visualize?
questions.textNL: Wat visualiseert een Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD)?
questions.question: Wat visualiseert een Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD)?
questions.questionEN: What does a Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD) visualize?
options: WIP, lead time, throughput, and bottlenecks
optionsEN: WIP, lead time, throughput, and bottlenecks
optionsNL: WIP, lead time, throughput en bottlenecks
questions.explanation: The cumulative flow diagram shows WIP, lead time, throughput, and bottlenecks all in one visualization.
questions.explanationNL: De CFD toont WIP, lead time, throughput en bottlenecks in één visualisatie.

questions.text: What is the typical flow efficiency in knowledge work?
questions.textNL: Wat is typisch de flow efficiency in kenniswerk?
questions.question: Wat is typisch de flow efficiency in kenniswerk?
questions.questionEN: What is the typical flow efficiency in knowledge work?
questions.explanation: Flow efficiency in knowledge work is typically 5-15%, meaning 85-95% of lead time is waiting time rather than active work.
questions.explanationNL: Flow efficiency in kenniswerk is typisch 5-15%, wat betekent dat 85-95% wachttijd is.
